Class TimePicker

java.lang.Object
javafx.scene.Node
javafx.scene.Parent
javafx.scene.layout.Region
javafx.scene.control.Control
javafx.scene.control.ComboBoxBase<LocalTime>
com.dlsc.gemsfx.CustomComboBox<LocalTime>
com.dlsc.gemsfx.TimePicker
All Implemented Interfaces:
javafx.css.Styleable, javafx.event.EventTarget, javafx.scene.control.Skinnable
public class TimePicker extends CustomComboBox<LocalTime>
A control for letting the user enter a time of day (see LocalTime). The control can be configured to only enter a time within a given time range. It can also be configured to show hours and minutes, or hours and minutes and seconds, or hours and minutes and seconds and milliseconds (see formatProperty())

  • Nested Class Summary

    Nested Classes
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    static enum 
    TimePicker.ClockType
    The time picker control supports 12 and 24 hour times. 12 hour times require an additional field for the user to enter am / pm.
    static enum 
    TimePicker.Format
    An enum for supported time units.

    Nested classes/interfaces inherited from class CustomComboBox

    CustomComboBox.ButtonDisplay

    final javafx.beans.property.BooleanProperty

    A property used to control whether the fields should automatically increase or decrease the previous field when they reach their upper or lower limit.

    The node that will be placed between the minutes and the seconds field.

    This consumer will be invoked to bring up a control for entering the time without using the keyboard.

    A flag used to signal whether the time fields should start at the beginning of its value range when it reaches the end of it.

    The node that will be placed between the seconds and the milliseconds field.

    final javafx.beans.property.BooleanProperty
    showPopupTriggerButton
    Determines if the control will show a button for showing or hiding the popup.
    final javafx.beans.property.IntegerProperty
    stepRateInMinutes
    Stores the "step rate" used by the control when the user increases or decreases the minutes field.
    final javafx.beans.property.ObjectProperty<LocalTime>
    time
    Stores the current time displayed by the control.

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    final void
    adjust()
    Adjusts the time picker's time based on the earliest and latest time allowed and also based on the step rate currently in effect.

  • Property Details

    • adjusted

      public final javafx.beans.property.ReadOnlyBooleanProperty adjustedProperty
      See Also:
      • isAdjusted()
      • adjustedProperty()

    • earliestTime

      public final javafx.beans.property.ObjectProperty<LocalTime> earliestTimeProperty
      The earliest time that the user can enter via the time picker.
      See Also:
      • getEarliestTime()
      • setEarliestTime(LocalTime)
      • earliestTimeProperty()

    • latestTime

      public final javafx.beans.property.ObjectProperty<LocalTime> latestTimeProperty
      The latest time that the user can enter via the time picker.
      See Also:
      • getLatestTime()
      • setLatestTime(LocalTime)
      • latestTimeProperty()

    • separator

      @Deprecated public final javafx.beans.property.ObjectProperty<javafx.scene.Node> separatorProperty
      The node that will be placed between the hours and the minutes field. The default separator is a label with text ":".
      See Also:
      • getSeparator()
      • setSeparator(Node)
      • separatorProperty()

    • hoursSeparator

      public final javafx.beans.property.ObjectProperty<javafx.scene.Node> hoursSeparatorProperty
      The node that will be placed between the hours and the minutes field. The default separator is a label with text ":".
      See Also:
      • getHoursSeparator()
      • setHoursSeparator(Node)
      • hoursSeparatorProperty()

    • showPopupTriggerButton

      public final javafx.beans.property.BooleanProperty showPopupTriggerButtonProperty
      Determines if the control will show a button for showing or hiding the popup.

      Can be set via CSS using the -fx-show-popup-trigger-button property. Valid values are: true or false. The default value is true.

      See Also:
      • isShowPopupTriggerButton()
      • setShowPopupTriggerButton(boolean)
      • showPopupTriggerButtonProperty()

    • time

      public final javafx.beans.property.ObjectProperty<LocalTime> timeProperty
      Stores the current time displayed by the control.
      See Also:
      • getTime()
      • setTime(LocalTime)
      • timeProperty()

    • stepRateInMinutes

      public final javafx.beans.property.IntegerProperty stepRateInMinutesProperty
      Stores the "step rate" used by the control when the user increases or decreases the minutes field. The step rate can be used to (for example) make the minutes increase or decrease by 15 minutes every time the user presses the arrow up or down keys.

      Can be set via CSS using the -fx-step-rate-in-minutes property. Valid values are: integers between 1 and 60 (inclusive). The default value is 1.

      See Also:
      • getStepRateInMinutes()
      • setStepRateInMinutes(int)
      • stepRateInMinutesProperty()

    • clockType

      public final javafx.beans.property.ObjectProperty<TimePicker.ClockType> clockTypeProperty
      The clock type determines whether the control will display 24 or 12 hours. If the control shows 12 hours then an additional field for choosing between the "am" or "pm" meridian will be added.

      Can be set via CSS using the -fx-clock-type property. Valid values are: TWENTY_FOUR_HOUR_CLOCK, TWELVE_HOUR_CLOCK. The default value is TWENTY_FOUR_HOUR_CLOCK.

      See Also:
      • getClockType()
      • setClockType(TimePicker.ClockType)
      • clockTypeProperty()

    • linkingFields

      public final javafx.beans.property.BooleanProperty linkingFieldsProperty
      A property used to control whether the fields should automatically increase or decrease the previous field when they reach their upper or lower limit.

      Can be set via CSS using the -fx-linking-fields property. Valid values are: true or false. The default value is true.

      See Also:
      • isLinkingFields()
      • setLinkingFields(boolean)
      • linkingFieldsProperty()

    • rollover

      public final javafx.beans.property.BooleanProperty rolloverProperty
      A flag used to signal whether the time fields should start at the beginning of its value range when it reaches the end of it. E.g. incrementing hour 23 would result in hour 0 when the user tries to increase it by one.

      Can be set via CSS using the -fx-rollover property. Valid values are: true or false. The default value is true.

      See Also:
      • isRollover()
      • setRollover(boolean)
      • rolloverProperty()

    • onShowPopup

      public final javafx.beans.property.ObjectProperty<Consumer<TimePicker>> onShowPopupProperty
      This consumer will be invoked to bring up a control for entering the time without using the keyboard. The default implementation shows a popup.
      See Also:
      • getOnShowPopup()
      • setOnShowPopup(Consumer)
      • onShowPopupProperty()

    • format

      public final javafx.beans.property.ObjectProperty<TimePicker.Format> formatProperty
      The format used by the picker, e.g. "hours and minutes", or "hours, minutes, and seconds".

      Can be set via CSS using the -fx-format property. Valid values are: HOURS_MINUTES, HOURS_MINUTES_SECONDS, HOURS_MINUTES_SECONDS_MILLIS. The default value is HOURS_MINUTES.

      See Also:
      • getFormat()
      • setFormat(TimePicker.Format)
      • formatProperty()

    • minutesSeparator

      public final javafx.beans.property.ObjectProperty<javafx.scene.Node> minutesSeparatorProperty
      The node that will be placed between the minutes and the seconds field. The default separator is a label with text ":".
      See Also:
      • getMinutesSeparator()
      • setMinutesSeparator(Node)
      • minutesSeparatorProperty()

    • secondsSeparator

      public final javafx.beans.property.ObjectProperty<javafx.scene.Node> secondsSeparatorProperty
      The node that will be placed between the seconds and the milliseconds field. The default separator is a label with text ".".
      See Also:
      • getSecondsSeparator()
      • setSecondsSeparator(Node)
      • secondsSeparatorProperty()

  • Constructor Details

    • TimePicker

      public TimePicker()
      Constructs a new time picker.

  • Method Details

    • isAdjusted

    • getEarliestTime

      public final LocalTime getEarliestTime()
      Gets the value of the earliestTime property.
      Property description:
      The earliest time that the user can enter via the time picker.
      Returns:
      the value of the earliestTime property
      See Also:
      • setEarliestTime(LocalTime)
      • earliestTimeProperty()

    • earliestTimeProperty

      public final javafx.beans.property.ObjectProperty<LocalTime> earliestTimeProperty()
      The earliest time that the user can enter via the time picker.
      Returns:
      the earliest time allowed
      See Also:
      • getEarliestTime()
      • setEarliestTime(LocalTime)

      The latest time that the user can enter via the time picker.

    • getSeparator

      @Deprecated public final javafx.scene.Node getSeparator()
      Deprecated.
      Gets the value of the separator property.
      Property description:
      The node that will be placed between the hours and the minutes field. The default separator is a label with text ":".
      Returns:
      the value of the separator property
      See Also:
      • setSeparator(Node)
      • separatorProperty()

    • separatorProperty

      @Deprecated public final javafx.beans.property.ObjectProperty<javafx.scene.Node> separatorProperty()
      Deprecated.
      The node that will be placed between the hours and the minutes field. The default separator is a label with text ":".
      Returns:
      a node used as a hoursSeparator
      See Also:
      • getSeparator()
      • setSeparator(Node)

    • getHoursSeparator

      public final javafx.scene.Node getHoursSeparator()
      Gets the value of the hoursSeparator property.
      Property description:
      The node that will be placed between the hours and the minutes field. The default separator is a label with text ":".
      Returns:
      the value of the hoursSeparator property
      See Also:
      • setHoursSeparator(Node)
      • hoursSeparatorProperty()

    • hoursSeparatorProperty

      public final javafx.beans.property.ObjectProperty<javafx.scene.Node> hoursSeparatorProperty()

      Returns:
      a node used as a hoursSeparator

    • isShowPopupTriggerButton

      public final boolean isShowPopupTriggerButton()
      Gets the value of the showPopupTriggerButton property.
      Property description:
      Determines if the control will show a button for showing or hiding the popup.

      Can be set via CSS using the -fx-show-popup-trigger-button property. Valid values are: true or false. The default value is true.

      Returns:
      the value of the showPopupTriggerButton property
      See Also:
      • setShowPopupTriggerButton(boolean)
      • showPopupTriggerButtonProperty()

    • showPopupTriggerButtonProperty

      public final javafx.beans.property.BooleanProperty showPopupTriggerButtonProperty()
      Determines if the control will show a button for showing or hiding the popup.

      Can be set via CSS using the -fx-show-popup-trigger-button property. Valid values are: true or false. The default value is true.

      Returns:
      true if the control will show a button for showing the popup
      See Also:
      • isShowPopupTriggerButton()
      • setShowPopupTriggerButton(boolean)

    • setShowPopupTriggerButton

      public final void setShowPopupTriggerButton(boolean showPopupTriggerButton)
      Sets the value of the showPopupTriggerButton property.
      Property description:
      Determines if the control will show a button for showing or hiding the popup.

      Can be set via CSS using the -fx-show-popup-trigger-button property. Valid values are: true or false. The default value is true.

      Parameters:
      showPopupTriggerButton - the value for the showPopupTriggerButton property
      See Also:
      • isShowPopupTriggerButton()
      • showPopupTriggerButtonProperty()

    • adjust

      public final void adjust()
      Adjusts the time picker's time based on the earliest and latest time allowed and also based on the step rate currently in effect.

    • getTime

      public final LocalTime getTime()
      Gets the value of the time property.
      Property description:
      Stores the current time displayed by the control.
      Returns:
      the value of the time property
      See Also:
      • setTime(LocalTime)
      • timeProperty()

    • timeProperty

      public final javafx.beans.property.ObjectProperty<LocalTime> timeProperty()
      Stores the current time displayed by the control.
      Returns:
      the chosen time
      See Also:
      • getTime()
      • setTime(LocalTime)

    • setTime

      public final void setTime(LocalTime time)
      Sets the value of the time property.
      Property description:
      Stores the current time displayed by the control.
      Parameters:
      time - the value for the time property
      See Also:
      • getTime()
      • timeProperty()

    • getStepRateInMinutes

      public final int getStepRateInMinutes()
      Gets the value of the stepRateInMinutes property.
      Property description:
      Stores the "step rate" used by the control when the user increases or decreases the minutes field. The step rate can be used to (for example) make the minutes increase or decrease by 15 minutes every time the user presses the arrow up or down keys.

      Can be set via CSS using the -fx-step-rate-in-minutes property. Valid values are: integers between 1 and 60 (inclusive). The default value is 1.

      Returns:
      the value of the stepRateInMinutes property
      See Also:
      • setStepRateInMinutes(int)
      • stepRateInMinutesProperty()

    • stepRateInMinutesProperty

      public final javafx.beans.property.IntegerProperty stepRateInMinutesProperty()
      Stores the "step rate" used by the control when the user increases or decreases the minutes field. The step rate can be used to (for example) make the minutes increase or decrease by 15 minutes every time the user presses the arrow up or down keys.

      Can be set via CSS using the -fx-step-rate-in-minutes property. Valid values are: integers between 1 and 60 (inclusive). The default value is 1.

      Returns:
      the step rate in minutes
      See Also:
      • getStepRateInMinutes()
      • setStepRateInMinutes(int)

    • setStepRateInMinutes

      public final void setStepRateInMinutes(int stepRateInMinutes)
      Sets the value of the stepRateInMinutes property.
      Property description:
      Stores the "step rate" used by the control when the user increases or decreases the minutes field. The step rate can be used to (for example) make the minutes increase or decrease by 15 minutes every time the user presses the arrow up or down keys.

      Can be set via CSS using the -fx-step-rate-in-minutes property. Valid values are: integers between 1 and 60 (inclusive). The default value is 1.

      Parameters:
      stepRateInMinutes - the value for the stepRateInMinutes property
      See Also:
      • getStepRateInMinutes()
      • stepRateInMinutesProperty()

    • getClockType

      public final TimePicker.ClockType getClockType()
      Gets the value of the clockType property.
      Property description:
      The clock type determines whether the control will display 24 or 12 hours. If the control shows 12 hours then an additional field for choosing between the "am" or "pm" meridian will be added.

      Can be set via CSS using the -fx-clock-type property. Valid values are: TWENTY_FOUR_HOUR_CLOCK, TWELVE_HOUR_CLOCK. The default value is TWENTY_FOUR_HOUR_CLOCK.

      Returns:
      the value of the clockType property
      See Also:
      • setClockType(TimePicker.ClockType)
      • clockTypeProperty()

    • clockTypeProperty

      public final javafx.beans.property.ObjectProperty<TimePicker.ClockType> clockTypeProperty()
      The clock type determines whether the control will display 24 or 12 hours. If the control shows 12 hours then an additional field for choosing between the "am" or "pm" meridian will be added.

      Can be set via CSS using the -fx-clock-type property. Valid values are: TWENTY_FOUR_HOUR_CLOCK, TWELVE_HOUR_CLOCK. The default value is TWENTY_FOUR_HOUR_CLOCK.

      Returns:
      the type of the clock
      See Also:
      • getClockType()
      • setClockType(TimePicker.ClockType)

    • setClockType

      public final void setClockType(TimePicker.ClockType clockType)
      Sets the value of the clockType property.
      Property description:
      The clock type determines whether the control will display 24 or 12 hours. If the control shows 12 hours then an additional field for choosing between the "am" or "pm" meridian will be added.

      Can be set via CSS using the -fx-clock-type property. Valid values are: TWENTY_FOUR_HOUR_CLOCK, TWELVE_HOUR_CLOCK. The default value is TWENTY_FOUR_HOUR_CLOCK.

      Parameters:
      clockType - the value for the clockType property
      See Also:
      • getClockType()
      • clockTypeProperty()

    • isLinkingFields

      public final boolean isLinkingFields()
      Gets the value of the linkingFields property.
      Property description:
      A property used to control whether the fields should automatically increase or decrease the previous field when they reach their upper or lower limit.

      Can be set via CSS using the -fx-linking-fields property. Valid values are: true or false. The default value is true.

      Returns:
      the value of the linkingFields property
      See Also:
      • setLinkingFields(boolean)
      • linkingFieldsProperty()

    • linkingFieldsProperty

      public final javafx.beans.property.BooleanProperty linkingFieldsProperty()
      A property used to control whether the fields should automatically increase or decrease the previous field when they reach their upper or lower limit.

      Can be set via CSS using the -fx-linking-fields property. Valid values are: true or false. The default value is true.

      Returns:
      true if linking fields is enabled
      See Also:
      • isLinkingFields()
      • setLinkingFields(boolean)

    • setLinkingFields

      public final void setLinkingFields(boolean linkingFields)
      Sets the value of the linkingFields property.
      Property description:
      A property used to control whether the fields should automatically increase or decrease the previous field when they reach their upper or lower limit.

      Can be set via CSS using the -fx-linking-fields property. Valid values are: true or false. The default value is true.

      Parameters:
      linkingFields - the value for the linkingFields property
      See Also:
      • isLinkingFields()
      • linkingFieldsProperty()

    • isRollover

      public final boolean isRollover()
      Gets the value of the rollover property.
      Property description:
      A flag used to signal whether the time fields should start at the beginning of its value range when it reaches the end of it. E.g. incrementing hour 23 would result in hour 0 when the user tries to increase it by one.

      Can be set via CSS using the -fx-rollover property. Valid values are: true or false. The default value is true.

      Returns:
      the value of the rollover property
      See Also:
      • setRollover(boolean)
      • rolloverProperty()

    • rolloverProperty

      public final javafx.beans.property.BooleanProperty rolloverProperty()
      A flag used to signal whether the time fields should start at the beginning of its value range when it reaches the end of it. E.g. incrementing hour 23 would result in hour 0 when the user tries to increase it by one.

      Can be set via CSS using the -fx-rollover property. Valid values are: true or false. The default value is true.

      Returns:
      true if the fields should rollover
      See Also:
      • isRollover()
      • setRollover(boolean)

    • setRollover

      public final void setRollover(boolean rollover)
      Sets the value of the rollover property.
      Property description:
      A flag used to signal whether the time fields should start at the beginning of its value range when it reaches the end of it. E.g. incrementing hour 23 would result in hour 0 when the user tries to increase it by one.

      Can be set via CSS using the -fx-rollover property. Valid values are: true or false. The default value is true.

      Parameters:
      rollover - the value for the rollover property
      See Also:
      • isRollover()
      • rolloverProperty()

    • getOnShowPopup

      public final Consumer<TimePicker> getOnShowPopup()
      Gets the value of the onShowPopup property.
      Property description:
      This consumer will be invoked to bring up a control for entering the time without using the keyboard. The default implementation shows a popup.
      Returns:
      the value of the onShowPopup property
      See Also:
      • setOnShowPopup(Consumer)
      • onShowPopupProperty()

    • onShowPopupProperty

      public final javafx.beans.property.ObjectProperty<Consumer<TimePicker>> onShowPopupProperty()
      This consumer will be invoked to bring up a control for entering the time without using the keyboard. The default implementation shows a popup.
      Returns:
      the "on show popup" consumer
      See Also:
      • getOnShowPopup()
      • setOnShowPopup(Consumer)

    • setOnShowPopup

      public final void setOnShowPopup(Consumer<TimePicker> onShowPopup)
      Sets the value of the onShowPopup property.
      Property description:
      This consumer will be invoked to bring up a control for entering the time without using the keyboard. The default implementation shows a popup.
      Parameters:
      onShowPopup - the value for the onShowPopup property
      See Also:
      • getOnShowPopup()
      • onShowPopupProperty()

    • formatProperty

      public final javafx.beans.property.ObjectProperty<TimePicker.Format> formatProperty()
      The format used by the picker, e.g. "hours and minutes", or "hours, minutes, and seconds".

      Can be set via CSS using the -fx-format property. Valid values are: HOURS_MINUTES, HOURS_MINUTES_SECONDS, HOURS_MINUTES_SECONDS_MILLIS. The default value is HOURS_MINUTES.

      Returns:
      the time format
      See Also:
      • getFormat()
      • setFormat(TimePicker.Format)

    • getFormat

      public final TimePicker.Format getFormat()
      Gets the value of the format property.
      Property description:
      The format used by the picker, e.g. "hours and minutes", or "hours, minutes, and seconds".

      Can be set via CSS using the -fx-format property. Valid values are: HOURS_MINUTES, HOURS_MINUTES_SECONDS, HOURS_MINUTES_SECONDS_MILLIS. The default value is HOURS_MINUTES.

      Returns:
      the value of the format property
      See Also:
      • setFormat(TimePicker.Format)
      • formatProperty()

    • setFormat

      public final void setFormat(TimePicker.Format timeUnit)
      Sets the value of the format property.
      Property description:
      The format used by the picker, e.g. "hours and minutes", or "hours, minutes, and seconds".

      Can be set via CSS using the -fx-format property. Valid values are: HOURS_MINUTES, HOURS_MINUTES_SECONDS, HOURS_MINUTES_SECONDS_MILLIS. The default value is HOURS_MINUTES.

      Parameters:
      timeUnit - the value for the format property
      See Also:
      • getFormat()
      • formatProperty()

    • getSecondsSeparator

      public final javafx.scene.Node getSecondsSeparator()
      Gets the value of the secondsSeparator property.
      Property description:
      The node that will be placed between the seconds and the milliseconds field. The default separator is a label with text ".".
      Returns:
      the value of the secondsSeparator property
      See Also:
      • setSecondsSeparator(Node)
      • secondsSeparatorProperty()

      Returns:
      a node used as a hoursSeparator
      See Also:

      • setSecondsSeparator(Node)
